row.name: All Websites dashboard
row.documentation: This report gives you an informational overview for each of your websites, containing the most general metrics about your visitors.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: If a visitor comes to your website for the first time or if they visit a page more than 30 minutes after their last page view, this will be recorded as a new visit.
metricsDocumentation.nb_actions: The number of actions performed by your visitors. Actions can be page views, internal site searches, downloads or outlinks.
metricsDocumentation.nb_pageviews: The number of times this page was visited.
metricsDocumentation.hits: The total count of all interactions recorded on your website, including page views, link clicks, downloads, and events.

row.name: Single Website dashboard
row.documentation: This report gives you an informational overview for a specific website, containing the most general metrics about your visitors.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: If a visitor comes to your website for the first time or if they visit a page more than 30 minutes after their last page view, this will be recorded as a new visit.
metricsDocumentation.nb_actions: The number of actions performed by your visitors. Actions can be page views, internal site searches, downloads or outlinks.
metricsDocumentation.nb_pageviews: The number of times this page was visited.
metricsDocumentation.hits: The total count of all interactions recorded on your website, including page views, link clicks, downloads, and events.

row.name: Visits Summary
row.documentation: This report provides a very general overview of how your visitors behave.

metricsDocumentation.nb_uniq_visitors: The number of unduplicated visitors coming to your website. Every user is only counted once, even if they visit the website multiple times a day.
metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: If a visitor comes to your website for the first time or if they visit a page more than 30 minutes after their last page view, this will be recorded as a new visit.
metricsDocumentation.nb_users: The number of users logged in your website. It is the number of unique active users that have a User ID set (via the Tracking code function 'setUserId').
metricsDocumentation.nb_actions: The number of actions performed by your visitors. Actions can be page views, internal site searches, downloads or outlinks.
metricsDocumentation.bounce_rate: The percentage of visits that only had a single pageview. This means, that the visitor left the website directly from the entrance page.
metricsDocumentation.nb_actions_per_visit: The average number of actions (page views, site searches, downloads or outlinks) that were performed during the visits.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_on_site: The average duration of a visit.

row.name: Country
row.documentation: Shows which country your visitors connected from when accessing your website.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: If a visitor comes to your website for the first time or if they visit a page more than 30 minutes after their last page view, this will be recorded as a new visit.
metricsDocumentation.nb_uniq_visitors: The number of unduplicated visitors coming to your website. Every user is only counted once, even if they visit the website multiple times a day.
metricsDocumentation.nb_actions: The number of actions performed by your visitors. Actions can be page views, internal site searches, downloads or outlinks.
metricsDocumentation.nb_users: The number of users logged in your website. It is the number of unique active users that have a User ID set (via the Tracking code function 'setUserId').
metricsDocumentation.nb_actions_per_visit: The average number of actions (page views, site searches, downloads or outlinks) that were performed during the visits.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_on_site: The average duration of a visit.
metricsDocumentation.bounce_rate: The percentage of visits that only had a single pageview. This means, that the visitor left the website directly from the entrance page.
metricsDocumentation.conversion_rate: The percentage of visits that triggered a conversion. The conversion rate is calculated using the number of visits that converted at least one goal. Visits converting multiple goals are only counted once in the conversion rate.

row.name: Continent
row.documentation: Shows which continent your visitors connected from when accessing your website.

metricsDocumentation.nb_uniq_visitors: The number of unduplicated visitors coming to your website. Every user is only counted once, even if they visit the website multiple times a day.

metricsDocumentation.bounce_rate: The percentage of visits that only had a single pageview. This means, that the visitor left the website directly from the entrance page.
metricsDocumentation.conversion_rate: The percentage of visits that triggered a conversion. The conversion rate is calculated using the number of visits that converted at least one goal. Visits converting multiple goals are only counted once in the conversion rate.

row.name: Region
row.documentation:
Shows which region your visitors connected from when accessing website.
Set up GeoIP in the Geolocation admin tab to provide data for this report. The commercial MaxMind GeoIP databases are more accurate than the gratis ones. Click here to see how accurate they are.

row.documentation: Shows which exact language code the visitor's browsers is set to. (e.g. "German — Austria (de-at)")

row.name: Device type
row.documentation: This report shows the types of devices your visitors were using. This report will always show all device types Matomo is able to detect, even if there were no visits with a specific type.

row.name: Browser engines
row.documentation:
This report shows your visitors' browsers broken down into browser engines.
The most important information for web developers is what kind of rendering engine their visitors are using. The labels contain the names of the engines followed by the most common browser using that engine in brackets.

row.name: Browser Plugins
row.documentation: This report shows which browser plugins your visitors had enabled. This information might be important for choosing the right way to deliver your content.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: If a visitor comes to your website for the first time or if they visit a page more than 30 minutes after their last page view, this will be recorded as a new visit.
metricsDocumentation.nb_visits_percentage: The percentage of total visits where the plugin was detected. A visit may include multiple plugins. The value is rounded down to whole numbers.

row.documentation: This graph shows what time it was in the visitors' time zones during their visits.

row.name: Custom Variables
row.documentation:
This report contains information about your Custom Variables. Click on a variable name to see the distribution of the values.
For more information about Custom Variables in general, read the Custom Variables documentation on matomo.org

metricsDocumentation.nb_actions_per_visit: The average number of actions (page views, site searches, downloads or outlinks) that were performed during the visits.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_on_site: The average duration of a visit.
metricsDocumentation.bounce_rate: The percentage of visits that only had a single pageview. This means, that the visitor left the website directly from the entrance page.
metricsDocumentation.conversion_rate: The percentage of visits that triggered a conversion. The conversion rate is calculated using the number of visits that converted at least one goal. Visits converting multiple goals are only counted once in the conversion rate.

row.name: Actions - Main metrics
row.documentation: This report provides a very basic overview of what actions your visitors take on your website.

metricsDocumentation.nb_pageviews: The number of times this page was visited.
metricsDocumentation.nb_uniq_pageviews: The number of visits that included this page. If a page was viewed multiple times during one visit, it is only counted once.
metricsDocumentation.nb_downloads: The number of times this link was clicked.
metricsDocumentation.nb_uniq_downloads: The number of visits that involved a click on this link. If a link was clicked multiple times during one visit, it is only counted once.
metricsDocumentation.nb_outlinks: The number of times this link was clicked.
metricsDocumentation.nb_uniq_outlinks: The number of visits that involved a click on this link. If a link was clicked multiple times during one visit, it is only counted once.
metricsDocumentation.nb_searches: The number of visits that searched for this keyword on your website's search engine.
metricsDocumentation.hits: The total count of all interactions recorded on your website, including page views, link clicks, downloads, and events.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_generation: The average time it took to generate the page. This metric includes the time it took the server to generate the web page, plus the time it took for the visitor to download the response from the server. A lower 'Avg. generation time' means a faster website for your visitors!

row.name: Page URLs
row.documentation:
This report contains information about the page URLs that have been visited.
The table is organized hierarchically, the URLs are displayed as a folder structure.
Use the plus and minus icons on the left to navigate.

metricsDocumentation.nb_hits: The number of times this page was visited.
metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: The number of visits that included this page. If a page was viewed multiple times during one visit, it is only counted once.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_on_page: The average amount of time visitors spent on this page (only the page, not the entire website).
metricsDocumentation.bounce_rate: The percentage of visits that started on this page and left the website straight away.
metricsDocumentation.exit_rate: The percentage of visits that left the website after viewing this page.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_generation: The average time it took to generate the page. This metric includes the time it took the server to generate the web page, plus the time it took for the visitor to download the response from the server. A lower 'Avg. generation time' means a faster website for your visitors!

row.name: Entry pages
row.documentation:
This report contains information about the entry pages that were used during the specified period. An entry page is the first page that a user views during their visit.
The entry URLs are displayed as a folder structure.
Use the plus and minus icons on the left to navigate.

metricsDocumentation.entry_nb_visits: Number of visits that started on this page.
metricsDocumentation.entry_bounce_count: Number of visits that started and ended on this page. This means that the visitor left the website after viewing only this page.
metricsDocumentation.bounce_rate: The percentage of visits that started on this page and left the website straight away.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_generation: The average time it took to generate the page. This metric includes the time it took the server to generate the web page, plus the time it took for the visitor to download the response from the server. A lower 'Avg. generation time' means a faster website for your visitors!

row.name: Entry page titles
row.documentation: This report contains information about the titles of entry pages that were used during the specified period. Use the plus and minus icons on the left to navigate.

metricsDocumentation.entry_nb_visits: Number of visits that started on this page.
metricsDocumentation.entry_bounce_count: Number of visits that started and ended on this page. This means that the visitor left the website after viewing only this page.
metricsDocumentation.bounce_rate: The percentage of visits that started on this page and left the website straight away.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_generation: The average time it took to generate the page. This metric includes the time it took the server to generate the web page, plus the time it took for the visitor to download the response from the server. A lower 'Avg. generation time' means a faster website for your visitors!

row.name: Exit pages
row.documentation:
This report contains information about the exit pages that occurred during the specified period. An exit page is the last page that a user views during their visit.
The exit URLs are displayed as a folder structure.
Use the plus and minus icons on the left to navigate.

metricsDocumentation.exit_nb_visits: Number of visits that ended on this page.
metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: The number of visits that included this page. If a page was viewed multiple times during one visit, it is only counted once.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_on_page: The average amount of time visitors spent on this page (only the page, not the entire website).
metricsDocumentation.bounce_rate: The percentage of visits that only had a single pageview. This means, that the visitor left the website directly from the entrance page.
metricsDocumentation.exit_rate: The percentage of visits that left the website after viewing this page.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_generation: The average time it took to generate the page. This metric includes the time it took the server to generate the web page, plus the time it took for the visitor to download the response from the server. A lower 'Avg. generation time' means a faster website for your visitors!

row.name: Exit page titles
row.documentation: This report contains information about the titles of exit pages that occurred during the specified period. Use the plus and minus icons on the left to navigate.

metricsDocumentation.exit_nb_visits: Number of visits that ended on this page.
metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: The number of visits that included this page. If a page was viewed multiple times during one visit, it is only counted once.
metricsDocumentation.exit_rate: The percentage of visits that left the website after viewing this page.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_generation: The average time it took to generate the page. This metric includes the time it took the server to generate the web page, plus the time it took for the visitor to download the response from the server. A lower 'Avg. generation time' means a faster website for your visitors!

row.name: Page titles
row.documentation:
This report contains information about the titles of the pages that have been visited.
The page title is the HTML Tag that most browsers show in their window title.

metricsDocumentation.nb_hits: The number of times this page was visited.
metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: The number of visits that included this page. If a page was viewed multiple times during one visit, it is only counted once.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_on_page: The average amount of time visitors spent on this page (only the page, not the entire website).
metricsDocumentation.bounce_rate: The percentage of visits that started on this page and left the website straight away.
metricsDocumentation.exit_rate: The percentage of visits that left the website after viewing this page.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_generation: The average time it took to generate the page. This metric includes the time it took the server to generate the web page, plus the time it took for the visitor to download the response from the server. A lower 'Avg. generation time' means a faster website for your visitors!

row.name: Site Search Keywords
row.documentation:
This report lists the Search Keywords that visitors searched for on your internal Search Engine.

Tracking searches that visitors make on your website is a very effective way to learn more about what your audience is looking for, it can help find ideas for new content, new Ecommerce products that potential customers might be searching for, and generally improve the visitors' experience on your website.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: The number of visits that searched for this keyword on your website's search engine.
metricsDocumentation.nb_pages_per_search: Visitors will search on your website, and sometimes click "next" to view more results. This is the average number of search results pages viewed for this keyword.
metricsDocumentation.exit_rate: The percentage of visits that left the website after searching for this Keyword on your Site Search engine.

row.name: Pages Following a Site Search
row.documentation:
When visitors search on your website, they are looking for a particular page, content, product, or service. This report lists the pages that were clicked the most after an internal search. In other words, the list of pages the most searched for by visitors already on your website.
Use the plus and minus icons on the left to navigate.

metricsDocumentation.nb_hits_following_search: The number of times this Page was visited after a visitor did a search on your website, and clicked on this page in the search results.
metricsDocumentation.nb_hits: The number of times this page was visited.

row.name: Page Titles Following a Site Search
Search Keywords with No Results
row.documentation:
Tracking searches that visitors make on your website is a very effective way to learn more about what your audience is looking for, it can help find ideas for new content, new Ecommerce products that potential customers might be searching for, and generally improve the visitors' experience on your website.

This report lists the Search Keywords that did not return any Search result: maybe the search engine algorithm can be improved, or maybe your visitors are looking for content that is not (yet) on your website?

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: The number of visits that searched for this keyword on your website's search engine.
metricsDocumentation.exit_rate: The percentage of visits that left the website after searching for this Keyword on your Site Search engine.

row.name: Page Titles Following a Site Search
row.documentation:
When visitors search on your website, they are looking for a particular page, content, product, or service. This report lists the pages that were clicked the most after an internal search. In other words, the list of pages the most searched for by visitors already on your website.
Use the plus and minus icons on the left to navigate.

metricsDocumentation.nb_hits_following_search: The number of times this Page was visited after a visitor did a search on your website, and clicked on this page in the search results.
metricsDocumentation.nb_hits: The number of times this page was visited.

row.name: Search Categories
row.documentation:
This report lists the Categories that visitors selected when they made a Search on your website.
For example, Ecommerce websites typically have a "Category" selector so that visitors can restrict their searches to all products in a specific Category.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: The number of visits that searched for this keyword on your website's search engine.
metricsDocumentation.nb_pages_per_search: Visitors will search on your website, and sometimes click "next" to view more results. This is the average number of search results pages viewed for this keyword.
metricsDocumentation.exit_rate: The percentage of visits that left the website after searching for this Keyword on your Site Search engine.

row.name: Outlinks
row.documentation:
This report shows a hierarchical list of outlink URLs that were clicked by your visitors. An outlink is a link that leads the visitor away from your website (to another domain).
Use the plus and minus icons on the left to navigate.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: The number of visits that involved a click on this link. If a link was clicked multiple times during one visit, it is only counted once.
metricsDocumentation.nb_hits: The number of times this link was clicked.

row.name: Downloads
row.documentation:
In this report, you can see which files your visitors have downloaded.
What Matomo counts as a download is the click on a download link. Whether the download was completed or not isn't known to Matomo.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: The number of visits that involved a click on this link. If a link was clicked multiple times during one visit, it is only counted once.
metricsDocumentation.nb_hits: The number of times this link was clicked.

row.name: Event Categories
row.documentation: This report shows the categories of each tracked event and how many times they occurred. You can view the event actions and names that were tracked along with each event category in each row's subtable. You can change which is shown by changing the secondary dimension with the link at the bottom of the report.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: The total number of visits where at least one event was triggered.
metricsDocumentation.nb_uniq_visitors: The number of deduplicated visitors who triggered an event on your website. Every visitor is only counted once, even if they visit or trigger multiple events on the website.
metricsDocumentation.min_event_value: The minimum value for this event
metricsDocumentation.max_event_value: The maximum value for this event
metricsDocumentation.nb_events_with_value: Number of events where an Event value was set
metricsDocumentation.avg_event_value: The average of all values for this event

processedMetrics.avg_event_value: The average of all values for this event

row.name: Event Actions
row.documentation: This report shows you the number of times each event action occurred. You can view the event categories and names that were tracked along with each event action in the row's subtable. You can change which is shown by changing the secondary dimension with the link at the bottom of the report.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: The total number of visits where at least one event was triggered.
metricsDocumentation.nb_uniq_visitors: The number of deduplicated visitors who triggered an event on your website. Every visitor is only counted once, even if they visit or trigger multiple events on the website.
metricsDocumentation.min_event_value: The minimum value for this event
metricsDocumentation.max_event_value: The maximum value for this event
metricsDocumentation.nb_events_with_value: Number of events where an Event value was set
metricsDocumentation.avg_event_value: The average of all values for this event

processedMetrics.avg_event_value: The average of all values for this event

row.name: Event Names
row.documentation: This report shows you the names associated with each tracked event and how many times they occurred. You can view the event actions and categories that were tracked along with each event name in each row's subtable. You can change which is shown by changing the secondary dimension with the link at the bottom of the report.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: The total number of visits where at least one event was triggered.
metricsDocumentation.nb_uniq_visitors: The number of deduplicated visitors who triggered an event on your website. Every visitor is only counted once, even if they visit or trigger multiple events on the website.
metricsDocumentation.min_event_value: The minimum value for this event
metricsDocumentation.max_event_value: The maximum value for this event
metricsDocumentation.nb_events_with_value: Number of events where an Event value was set
metricsDocumentation.avg_event_value: The average of all values for this event

processedMetrics.avg_event_value: The average of all values for this event

row.name: Content Name
row.documentation: This report shows the names of the content your visitors viewed and interacted with.

metricsDocumentation.nb_impressions: The number of times a content block, such as a banner or an ad, was displayed on a page.
metricsDocumentation.nb_interactions: The number of times a content block was interacted with (eg, a 'click' on a banner or ad).
metricsDocumentation.interaction_rate: The ratio of content impressions to interactions.

row.name: Content Piece
row.documentation: This report shows the pieces of content your visitors viewed and interacted with.

metricsDocumentation.nb_impressions: The number of times a content block, such as a banner or an ad, was displayed on a page.
metricsDocumentation.nb_interactions: The number of times a content block was interacted with (eg, a 'click' on a banner or ad).
metricsDocumentation.interaction_rate: The ratio of content impressions to interactions.

row.name: Length of visits
row.documentation:
How many visits had a certain total duration. Initially, the report is shown as a tag cloud, wherein more common durations have a larger font.
Please note, that you can view the report in other ways than as a tag cloud. Use the controls at the bottom of the report to do so.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: If a visitor comes to your website for the first time or if they visit a page more than 30 minutes after their last page view, this will be recorded as a new visit.

row.name: Pages per visit
row.documentation:
How many visits involved a certain number of pageviews. Initially, the report is shown as a tag cloud, wherein more common numbers of pages have a larger font.
Please note, that you can view the report in other ways than as a tag cloud. Use the controls at the bottom of the report to do so.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: If a visitor comes to your website for the first time or if they visit a page more than 30 minutes after their last page view, this will be recorded as a new visit.

row.name: Visits by visit number
row.documentation:
The number of visitors reaching their Nth visit. I.e. the total amount of visitors visiting your website a given amount of times or more.
Please note, that you can view the report in other ways than as a tag cloud. Use the controls at the bottom of the report to do so.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: If a visitor comes to your website for the first time or if they visit a page more than 30 minutes after their last page view, this will be recorded as a new visit.
metricsDocumentation.nb_visits_percentage: The percentage of totals visits, where visitors had this amount of visits in total yet.

row.name: Visits by days since last visit
row.documentation: How many visits were from returning visitors whose last visit was a certain number of days ago.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: If a visitor comes to your website for the first time or if they visit a page more than 30 minutes after their last page view, this will be recorded as a new visit.

row.name: Returning Visits
row.documentation: This report shows general metrics like visits for returning visitors side by side with the same metrics for new visitors. Learn how returning visitors perform overall compared to new visitors.

metrics.max_actions_returning: Maximum actions in one returning visit

processedMetrics.avg_time_on_site_returning: Avg. Duration of a Returning Visit (in sec)
processedMetrics.avg_time_on_site_new: Avg. Duration of a New Visit (in sec)

row.name: Performance overview
row.documentation: This report provides an overview of how fast your webpages become visible to your visitors. This includes both how long it takes for browsers to download your webpages and how long it takes for browsers to display them.

metricsDocumentation.avg_time_network: Average time (in seconds) it takes to connect to the server. This includes the time needed to lookup DNS and establish a TCP connection. This value might be 0 after the first request to a domain as the browser might cache the connection.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_server: Average time (in seconds) it takes the server to generate the page. This is the time between the server receiving the request and starting to serve the response.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_transfer: Average time (in seconds) it takes the browser to download the response from the server. This is the time from receiving the first byte till the response is complete.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_dom_processing: Average time (in seconds) the browser spends loading the webpage after the response was fully received and before the user can start interacting with it.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_dom_completion: Average time (in seconds) it takes for the browser to load media and execute any Javascript code listening for the DOMContentLoaded event after the webpage was loaded and the user can already interact with it.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_on_load: Average time (in seconds) it takes the browser to execute Javascript code waiting for the window.load event. This event is triggered once the DOM has completely rendered.
metricsDocumentation.avg_page_load_time: Average time (in seconds) it takes from requesting a page until the page is fully rendered within the browser

row.name: Referrers Overview
row.documentation: This report shows what acquisition channels your visitors used to get to your website, and the number of visits each channel type is responsible for.

metrics.Referrers_visitorsFromSearchEngines_perc: Percent of Visitors from Search Engines
metrics.Referrers_visitorsFromSocialNetworks_per: Percent of Visitors from Social Networks
metrics.Referrers_visitorsFromAIAssistants_perce: Percent of Visitors from AI Assistants
metrics.Referrers_visitorsFromDirectEntry_percen: Percent of Visitors from Direct Entry

row.name: Channel Type
row.documentation:
This table contains information about the distribution of the channel types.
Direct Entry: A visitor has entered the URL in their browser and started browsing on your website - they entered the website directly.
Search Engines: A visitor was referred to your website by a search engine.
See the "Search Engines & Keywords" report for more details.
Websites: The visitor followed a link on another website that led to your site.
See the "Websites" report for more details.
Campaigns: Visitors that came to your website as the result of a campaign.
See the "Campaigns" report for more details.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: If a visitor comes to your website for the first time or if they visit a page more than 30 minutes after their last page view, this will be recorded as a new visit.
metricsDocumentation.nb_uniq_visitors: The number of unduplicated visitors coming to your website. Every user is only counted once, even if they visit the website multiple times a day.
metricsDocumentation.nb_actions: The number of actions performed by your visitors. Actions can be page views, internal site searches, downloads or outlinks.
metricsDocumentation.nb_users: The number of users logged in your website. It is the number of unique active users that have a User ID set (via the Tracking code function 'setUserId').
metricsDocumentation.nb_actions_per_visit: The average number of actions (page views, site searches, downloads or outlinks) that were performed during the visits.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_on_site: The average duration of a visit.
metricsDocumentation.bounce_rate: The percentage of visits that only had a single pageview. This means, that the visitor left the website directly from the entrance page.
metricsDocumentation.conversion_rate: The percentage of visits that triggered a conversion. The conversion rate is calculated using the number of visits that converted at least one goal. Visits converting multiple goals are only counted once in the conversion rate.

row.name: All Channels
row.documentation: This report shows all your Referrers in one unified report, listing all Websites, Search keywords and Campaigns used by your visitors to find your website.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: If a visitor comes to your website for the first time or if they visit a page more than 30 minutes after their last page view, this will be recorded as a new visit.
metricsDocumentation.nb_uniq_visitors: The number of unduplicated visitors coming to your website. Every user is only counted once, even if they visit the website multiple times a day.
metricsDocumentation.nb_actions: The number of actions performed by your visitors. Actions can be page views, internal site searches, downloads or outlinks.
metricsDocumentation.nb_users: The number of users logged in your website. It is the number of unique active users that have a User ID set (via the Tracking code function 'setUserId').

metricsDocumentation.conversion_rate: The percentage of visits that triggered a conversion. The conversion rate is calculated using the number of visits that converted at least one goal. Visits converting multiple goals are only counted once in the conversion rate.

row.name: Keywords
row.documentation:
This report shows which keywords users were searching for before they were referred to your website.

By clicking on a row in the table, you can see the distribution of search engines that were queried for the keyword.

Note: This report lists most keywords as not defined, because most search engines do not send the exact keyword used on the search engine.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: If a visitor comes to your website for the first time or if they visit a page more than 30 minutes after their last page view, this will be recorded as a new visit.

row.name: Search Engines
row.documentation:
This report shows which search engines referred users to your website.
By clicking on a row in the table, you can see what users were searching for using a specific search engine.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: If a visitor comes to your website for the first time or if they visit a page more than 30 minutes after their last page view, this will be recorded as a new visit.

row.name: Websites
row.documentation:
In this table, you can see which websites referred visitors to your site.
By clicking on a row in the table, you can see which URLs the links to your website were on.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: If a visitor comes to your website for the first time or if they visit a page more than 30 minutes after their last page view, this will be recorded as a new visit.
metricsDocumentation.nb_uniq_visitors: The number of unduplicated visitors coming to your website. Every user is only counted once, even if they visit the website multiple times a day.

row.name: Social Networks
row.documentation:
This report shows which social networks led visitors to your website.
By clicking on a row in the table, you can see from which social network pages visitors came to your website.

row.name: AI Assistants
row.documentation:
This report shows which AI assistants led visitors to your website.
By clicking on a row in the table, you can see from which AI assistant pages visitors came to your website.

row.name: Campaigns
row.documentation: This report shows which campaigns led visitors to your website.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: If a visitor comes to your website for the first time or if they visit a page more than 30 minutes after their last page view, this will be recorded as a new visit.
metricsDocumentation.nb_uniq_visitors: The number of unduplicated visitors coming to your website. Every user is only counted once, even if they visit the website multiple times a day.

metricsDocumentation.nb_users: The number of users logged in your website. It is the number of unique active users that have a User ID set (via the Tracking code function 'setUserId').
metricsDocumentation.nb_actions_per_visit: The average number of actions (page views, site searches, downloads or outlinks) that were performed during the visits.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_on_site: The average duration of a visit.
metricsDocumentation.bounce_rate: The percentage of visits that only had a single pageview. This means, that the visitor left the website directly from the entrance page.
metricsDocumentation.conversion_rate: The percentage of visits that triggered a conversion. The conversion rate is calculated using the number of visits that converted at least one goal. Visits converting multiple goals are only counted once in the conversion rate.

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits_converted: Number of visits where at least one goal was successfully converted.

metricsDocumentation.revenue: The total revenue generated by Product sales. Excludes tax, shipping and discount.
metricsDocumentation.quantity: Quantity is the total number of products sold for each Product Name.
metricsDocumentation.orders: The total number of Ecommerce orders which contained this Product Name at least once.
metricsDocumentation.avg_price: The average price for this Product Name.
metricsDocumentation.avg_quantity: The average quantity of this Product Name sold in Ecommerce orders.
metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: The number of visits on the Product/Category page. This is also used to process the Product Name conversion rate. This metric is in the report if Ecommerce view tracking was setup on Product/Category pages.
metricsDocumentation.conversion_rate: The Product Name conversion rate is the number of orders containing this product divided by number of visits on the product page.

metricsDocumentation.nb_conversions: The total number of goal conversions that occurred within each visit. Represents how many visits took place before converting.

metricsDocumentation.BotTracking_AIChatbotsRequests: Total number of bot requests recorded during the selected period. Includes both page and document URLs.
metricsDocumentation.BotTracking_AIChatbotsAcquiredVisits: Visits that started after someone clicked from an AI chatbot (Referrers → AI Assistants).
metricsDocumentation.BotTracking_AIChatbotsUniquePageUrls: Distinct page URLs requested by AI chatbots.
metricsDocumentation.BotTracking_AIChatbotsNotFoundRequests: Number of bot requests that returned an HTTP 404 or 410 response, including document URLs.
metricsDocumentation.BotTracking_AIChatbotsUniqueChatbots: AI chatbot user agents detected during the selected period.
metricsDocumentation.BotTracking_AIChatbotsUniqueDocumentUrls: Distinct download or document URLs requested by AI chatbots.
metricsDocumentation.BotTracking_AIChatbotsServerErrorRequest: Number of bot requests that returned an HTTP 5xx response, including document URLs.
metricsDocumentation.BotTracking_AIChatbotsClickThroughRate: Share of AI chatbot requests that resulted in acquired visits (acquired visits ÷ requests).

row.name: AI Chatbots
row.documentation: Discover which AI chatbots are crawling your site and how many hits each one generates. Expand a chatbot to review the most frequently requested pages or documents.

metricsDocumentation.requests: Total number of bot requests recorded during the selected period. Includes both page and document URLs.
metricsDocumentation.page_requests: Total number of bot requests to page URLs recorded during the selected period.
metricsDocumentation.document_requests: Total number of bot requests to document URLs recorded during the selected period.
metricsDocumentation.visits_acquired: Visits that started after someone clicked from an AI chatbot (Referrers → AI Assistants).

row.name: Pages
row.documentation: This report shows which HTML page URLs were accessed by AI Assistants (like ChatGPT) to retrieve real-time information during user chats. It helps you identify which parts of your website are being referenced by AI systems, offering insights into your site’s visibility in AI-assisted interactions and potential content opportunities. It focuses only on visits to standard web pages, excluding file downloads such as PDFs, Word, or Excel documents — which are covered in a separate report dedicated to document access. It’s important to note that none of these pages were actually viewed by humans in the traditional way — all requests originate from AI assistants fetching content automatically. Currently, these reports exclusively include requests from AI bots that do not execute JavaScript. They do not include traffic from AI crawlers used for training AI models or from AI agents capable of executing JavaScript.

metricsDocumentation.requests: Total number of bot requests to page URLs recorded during the selected period.
metricsDocumentation.server_error_5xx_requests: Number of bot requests that returned an HTTP 5xx (server error) response.
metricsDocumentation.page_not_found_404_requests: Number of bot requests that returned an HTTP 404 (Not Found) or 410 (Gone) response.
metricsDocumentation.avg_server_time: Average time the server took to respond to these requests. This relies on server or CDN tracking and may be empty if it is not configured, or empty for a URL when none of its requests carried this data.
metricsDocumentation.avg_response_size: Average size of the responses returned for these requests. This relies on server or CDN tracking and may be empty if it is not configured, or empty for a URL when none of its requests carried this data.

row.name: Documents
row.documentation: This report shows which document URLs were accessed by AI Chatbots (like ChatGPT) to retrieve real-time information during user chats. It helps you identify which parts of your website are being referenced by AI systems, offering insights into your site’s visibility in AI-assisted interactions and potential content opportunities. It focuses only on visits to documents and media files like PDF, Word, Excel and MP4, excluding HTML page URLs — which are covered in a separate report dedicated to pages. It’s important to note that none of these documents were actually viewed by humans in the traditional way — all requests originate from AI assistants fetching content automatically. Currently, these reports exclusively include requests from AI chatbots that do not execute JavaScript. They do not include traffic from AI crawlers used for training AI models or from AI agents capable of executing JavaScript.

metricsDocumentation.requests: Total number of bot requests to document URLs recorded during the selected period.
metricsDocumentation.server_error_5xx_requests: Number of bot requests that returned an HTTP 5xx (server error) response.
metricsDocumentation.page_not_found_404_requests: Number of bot requests that returned an HTTP 404 (Not Found) or 410 (Gone) response.
metricsDocumentation.avg_server_time: Average time the server took to respond to these requests. This relies on server or CDN tracking and may be empty if it is not configured, or empty for a URL when none of its requests carried this data.
metricsDocumentation.avg_response_size: Average size of the responses returned for these requests. This relies on server or CDN tracking and may be empty if it is not configured, or empty for a URL when none of its requests carried this data.

row.name: Broken Pages and Documents
row.documentation: This report lists broken page URLs and document URLs (such as PDFs, Word, or Excel files) that were accessed by AI Chatbots (like ChatGPT) but returned errors or were unavailable. It helps identify missing or inaccessible content that LLMs attempted to fetch, allowing you to fix broken links and improve your site’s reliability and discoverability.

metricsDocumentation.total_broken_requests: Number of bot requests that returned an HTTP 404 (Not Found), 410 (Gone), or 5xx (server error) response.
metricsDocumentation.server_error_5xx_requests: Number of bot requests that returned an HTTP 5xx (server error) response.
metricsDocumentation.page_not_found_404_requests: Number of bot requests that returned an HTTP 404 (Not Found) or 410 (Gone) response.

metrics.max_actions_ai_agent: Maximum Actions in AI Agent Visit
metrics.max_actions_human: Maximum Actions in one Human Visit

processedMetrics.avg_time_on_site_human: Avg. Duration of a Human Visit (in sec)

metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: If a visitor comes to your website for the first time or if they visit a page more than 30 minutes after their last page view, this will be recorded as a new visit.
metricsDocumentation.nb_uniq_visitors: The number of unduplicated visitors coming to your website. Every user is only counted once, even if they visit the website multiple times a day.
metricsDocumentation.nb_actions: The number of actions performed by your visitors. Actions can be page views, internal site searches, downloads or outlinks.

metricsDocumentation.conversion_rate: The percentage of visits that triggered a conversion. The conversion rate is calculated using the number of visits that converted at least one goal. Visits converting multiple goals are only counted once in the conversion rate.

row.name: Main metrics
row.documentation: This report gives an overview of the most important metrics across all categories and plugins.

metrics.Referrers_visitorsFromSearchEngines_perc: Percent of Visitors from Search Engines
metrics.Referrers_visitorsFromSocialNetworks_per: Percent of Visitors from Social Networks
metrics.Referrers_visitorsFromAIAssistants_perce: Percent of Visitors from AI Assistants
metrics.Referrers_visitorsFromDirectEntry_percen: Percent of Visitors from Direct Entry
metrics.max_actions_returning: Maximum actions in one returning visit
metrics.max_actions_ai_agent: Maximum Actions in AI Agent Visit
metrics.max_actions_human: Maximum Actions in one Human Visit

metricsDocumentation.nb_pageviews: The number of times this page was visited.
metricsDocumentation.nb_uniq_pageviews: The number of visits that included this page. If a page was viewed multiple times during one visit, it is only counted once.
metricsDocumentation.nb_downloads: The number of times this link was clicked.
metricsDocumentation.nb_uniq_downloads: The number of visits that involved a click on this link. If a link was clicked multiple times during one visit, it is only counted once.
metricsDocumentation.nb_outlinks: The number of times this link was clicked.
metricsDocumentation.nb_uniq_outlinks: The number of visits that involved a click on this link. If a link was clicked multiple times during one visit, it is only counted once.
metricsDocumentation.nb_searches: The number of visits that searched for this keyword on your website's search engine.
metricsDocumentation.hits: The total count of all interactions recorded on your website, including page views, link clicks, downloads, and events.
metricsDocumentation.nb_visits_converted: Number of visits where at least one goal was successfully converted.
metricsDocumentation.nb_uniq_visitors: The number of unduplicated visitors coming to your website. Every user is only counted once, even if they visit the website multiple times a day.
metricsDocumentation.nb_visits: If a visitor comes to your website for the first time or if they visit a page more than 30 minutes after their last page view, this will be recorded as a new visit.
metricsDocumentation.nb_users: The number of users logged in your website. It is the number of unique active users that have a User ID set (via the Tracking code function 'setUserId').
metricsDocumentation.nb_actions: The number of actions performed by your visitors. Actions can be page views, internal site searches, downloads or outlinks.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_network: Average time (in seconds) it takes to connect to the server. This includes the time needed to lookup DNS and establish a TCP connection. This value might be 0 after the first request to a domain as the browser might cache the connection.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_server: Average time (in seconds) it takes the server to generate the page. This is the time between the server receiving the request and starting to serve the response.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_transfer: Average time (in seconds) it takes the browser to download the response from the server. This is the time from receiving the first byte till the response is complete.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_dom_processing: Average time (in seconds) the browser spends loading the webpage after the response was fully received and before the user can start interacting with it.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_dom_completion: Average time (in seconds) it takes for the browser to load media and execute any Javascript code listening for the DOMContentLoaded event after the webpage was loaded and the user can already interact with it.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_on_load: Average time (in seconds) it takes the browser to execute Javascript code waiting for the window.load event. This event is triggered once the DOM has completely rendered.
metricsDocumentation.avg_page_load_time: Average time (in seconds) it takes from requesting a page until the page is fully rendered within the browser
metricsDocumentation.BotTracking_AIChatbotsRequests: Total number of bot requests recorded during the selected period. Includes both page and document URLs.
metricsDocumentation.BotTracking_AIChatbotsAcquiredVisits: Visits that started after someone clicked from an AI chatbot (Referrers → AI Assistants).
metricsDocumentation.BotTracking_AIChatbotsUniquePageUrls: Distinct page URLs requested by AI chatbots.
metricsDocumentation.BotTracking_AIChatbotsNotFoundRequests: Number of bot requests that returned an HTTP 404 or 410 response, including document URLs.
metricsDocumentation.BotTracking_AIChatbotsUniqueChatbots: AI chatbot user agents detected during the selected period.
metricsDocumentation.BotTracking_AIChatbotsUniqueDocumentUrls: Distinct download or document URLs requested by AI chatbots.
metricsDocumentation.BotTracking_AIChatbotsServerErrorRequest: Number of bot requests that returned an HTTP 5xx response, including document URLs.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_generation: The average time it took to generate the page. This metric includes the time it took the server to generate the web page, plus the time it took for the visitor to download the response from the server. A lower 'Avg. generation time' means a faster website for your visitors!
metricsDocumentation.conversion_rate: The percentage of visits that triggered a conversion. The conversion rate is calculated using the number of visits that converted at least one goal. Visits converting multiple goals are only counted once in the conversion rate.
metricsDocumentation.bounce_rate: The percentage of visits that only had a single pageview. This means, that the visitor left the website directly from the entrance page.
metricsDocumentation.nb_actions_per_visit: The average number of actions (page views, site searches, downloads or outlinks) that were performed during the visits.
metricsDocumentation.avg_time_on_site: The average duration of a visit.
metricsDocumentation.BotTracking_AIChatbotsClickThroughRate: Share of AI chatbot requests that resulted in acquired visits (acquired visits ÷ requests).

processedMetrics.avg_time_on_site_returning: Avg. Duration of a Returning Visit (in sec)
processedMetrics.avg_time_on_site_new: Avg. Duration of a New Visit (in sec)
processedMetrics.avg_time_on_site_ai_agent: Avg. Duration of an AI Agent Visit (in sec)
processedMetrics.nb_actions_per_visit_ai_agent: Avg. Actions per AI Agent Visit
processedMetrics.bounce_rate_ai_agent: Bounce Rate for AI Agent Visits
processedMetrics.avg_time_on_site_human: Avg. Duration of a Human Visit (in sec)
